reviews.review_description: This is not really a game. In fact any interactive part of the game was terrible. This is really a story that has meaning and a message, a very sad message. Personally I don't think this really translates well to VR and isn't something I would recommend to most people.

reviews.review_title: It made me cry! Not a game.
reviews.review_description: This is an immersive experience rather than a traditional game. I loved it; it's a clever way to make players feel the issue being explored. It's deeply emotional and brought me to tears. While there is some interaction, it's minimal, yet this does not diminish the enjoyment. Anything else I could say would spoil the story.

This is a symbolic narration, with no other interaction than taking and introducing a key, holding some enlightened soul balls, 3 of them, but you only have two hands. So you are helpless to sacrifice the 3rd soul to a monster. Wow. I could not even use a teleport. What a politic message on the topic of refugees, and what a poor statement that only a 10th of all countries accept refugees, when no statement at all is given why 90% of countries support one or the other country in conflict, making wars economically so profitable. After all, the thousands of refugee's we import are exploited  to keep many jobs underpaid. If you don't want this job, a refugee will have to take it to get his right to stay. Refugee's are modern versions of slavery. This story does not give a multi-perspective of the problem, so it must be considered as unilateral propaganda that deserves to be criticised.  Monsters are naive, childish, yes, the story is told from the perspective of a child, which makes it even more critiquable. The VR message : stay passive, you can't act anyway, have a tear and accept it, go on sleeping. I would prefer chewing bubble gum and kick a...
This game is a good game if you want to go through a world and listen to a story.
Great experience. Never expected to see something like this in a VR

Before it got to the end about the refugees, it was really interesting. Playing with the three balls, trying to protect them, the monsters, etc. it was all good. I already knew it wasn't a game, but it would make a fun game. After the balls get destroyed, you go on an adventure to bring them back or help the people in the line or something.

It has the potential to be a (fun) game.

reviews.review_title: This app is a five star plus
reviews.review_description: I give this app more than five stars, I love the story but the whole experience is just beautiful, I enjoy sharing the app with friends and family, I hope to see more like this one.
Give them their land back.

This is honestly an experience that made me understand something and helped me to empathize at a deeper and more personal level than any article or documentary before. It is not condescending or moralizing while doing so. The end, when you understand what actually happened before, was so far one of the most profound personal experiences I had in my few months with the quest 3 so far. I hope it lasts. Thanks to the developers.

Don't get spoilered. Download and watch/play this - it is free after all. Played it yesterday, I am still not over it (in a good way).

Update:
Back after months for the new content. Still crying when I think of The Key. It is the most wonderful and most immersing story I ever experienced. It will be with me forever.

At first I thought I must be missing something because it says in description you’ll have to make choices and choose the right key. I stood there forever while this woman talked. Literally only picked up one key so nothing to choose. Got to play with three balls for a minute then they were crushed and killed. Put on a mask in ocean and then went straight to the end to talk about refugees. I was really excited that I would be able to choose keys and make choices. It was weird. I was in the sky. Saw two ugly monsters, a row of aliens standing there then each la… straight to the end. Very disappointed. Description needs to be changed.
Just wow
This is the saddest game I have ever played and I started crying when all my companions die off
It's a proper eye opener. Kinda depressing too. Had no idea what it was about when it first started. Definitely worth a watch.

reviews.review_title: It helped me alter my own lense.
reviews.review_description: For those of you who don't get it, I get it. You have never lost all that you have, home, family friends and culture. The point of this isn't to make you sad, it is to hopefully bring you to connect on some level with what 100's of thousands of humans your own age are going through, past present and unfortunately future.

I don’t really get it at all because it’s just consistent at this point because I’ve played it like 30 times and every single time I did something different it just played the same story, oh all of the things die and you get like a transition to old times or something yeah, it doesn’t really make sense, it’s a really cool idea. I just think the different ones you pick there should be like a different story that’s all but I just feel like it could be a bit different.

Spoiler;
I was just in the mood to play a game to unwind.. and l happened to open it. It said it was an "experience game type", when l saw the "aqsa mosque" my eyes widened but l doubted myself and kept on listening and following the game,, then l burst into tears and couldn't stop for an hour after. The key the doesn't open a door forever after. The companions being lost. The loss of your entire future and belonging and life. And with everything going on on the world nowadays as well. Thank you for creating this game and the beautiful execution- the shift from the simplistic art style to the realistic flooring and environment at the end delived the proper shocking effect piercing throughone's heart. 🔻🇵🇸
Totally unplayable. Do not waste your money.

developer_response.body: The issue has been fixed, please try again :) And by the way, it's a free experience!

reviews.review_title: Couldn't be more relevant than today
reviews.review_description:
I didn't now what was the whole story about. The experience is very well made, with only a few interactions needed from the viewer.
Interesting little story until the truth unfolds. It should be a mandatory experience for everyone!

reviews.review_title: broke my heart in the best way
reviews.review_description: a short experience i’ve been thinking about for weeks. definitely worth a try

I expected a game, guess I was wrong. It's a sad story that was done really well with the exception that I could not reach or interact with some aspects of it as it told the story I was in a chair and did not know I could move until almost the end. Just k kw it is more of a 3D short film, and definitely not a game lol

developer_response.body: Hi there! We are sorry you were disappointed, however it says in the description " The Key is NOT a video game. So relax, and let the story guide you", precisely to avoid people expecting a video game :) As an experience we put a lot of effort in, and made available to everyone for free, we are saddened by your 1 star rating.

reviews.review_description: I had very high hopes for this story. It takes a very long time to download because it’s a very big file. The first 10 minutes have average animation IMO. After about 10 minutes, it cuts to the chase about the key and shows you a slideshow emphasizing the pain and real life faces of refugees and the struggles they encounter. It’s a part of reality but not what I’m looking for in VR. if this is your thing, I would recommend not going through the misery of this app. Simply turn on the news if that’s your style of buzz kill. Buzzkill score 10/10.
This is both beautiful and heartbreaking in equal measure. Well done to everyone involved in its creation.
I thought this is a great puzzle game. But instead, it’s just a propaganda

Not the most Interactive if you are going into this expecting a game like experience but it is easily one of the most Immersive. My only complaint is the length. Wish it was a multi episode mini series that I could give money too, fleshing out the lead up..  Beautiful art style, well performed narration, touching story.
Propaganda for refugees. Honestly thought it was a puzzle game. All you do is listen to how you cant save your friends, go in an elevator, but on a bubble helmet and then stand in rubble.
Total political propaganda in disguise as a VR experience. Didn't enjoy this at all.

I'm not surprised this won at Tribecca.   It's long, it's boring, and it's depressing.  Not appropriate if you want to escape to a virtual world.  I agree with a lot of the other reviewers that this is not appropriate for a virtual escape.  I've seen other films that have won at Tribecca, and most audiences don't like them.  This is what happens when a film festival is political.
A bit of a chore to watch until the last few minutes, I would rather have watched a documentary based on the last few minutes and just skip the visuals before it.
Total Waste of time, Even though it deals with an important issue - refugees
This is in no way shape or form a beautiful experience, it's a story with an agenda and one I'm not particularly interested in. I don't need my VR experience to be ruined by liberal propaganda.
This game is to slow and very boring as you can't do much.
More political preaching.. Spoiler alert it's refugees. Cause we don't know about them you see. What with us all being racists in all
It shows different scenes with nice graphics and later a message about refugees.
So touching! I’m new to VR and glad I got to experience this. I had a hard time focusing but it was because of my tears...

Do not play this game if you don't want to be mislead.


The game starts really atmospheric and has good story telling, but after 20 min. It turns out it was all a coping mechanism for a refugee.

Visually stunning artwork, poetically beautiful narration, and small bits of interaction to keep you involved in the story. The message is heartbreaking but inspiring, and I enjoyed this short film very much.

reviews.review_title: Nice start but becomes to political
reviews.review_description: This short story started nice and looked great....until the end when you see ruins of some Middle Eastern country and it becomes big political about refugees.  The idea is nice for school to teach young kids in a simple way about refugees....but not for VR.

I am lost for words of how meaningful this short game is, the meaning behind it is truly horrible. However it’s not just a narrative where you sit there the whole time, you can interact a bit.

Very well made!

The message of this blatantly politically charged experience is muddied at best and dangerous at worse. 

Despite its “documentary” format, it seems to fundamentally misunderstand the topic it’s discussing.
